title: Teacher Qualification Distribution And Deployment Equity Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping teacher-qualification distribution and deployment-equity gaps at sub-national scale in low-income countries, with explicit EMIS data-quality documentation, qualification-proxy limitations, and the distinction between teacher presence and teaching effectiveness.
why_it_matters: UNESCO estimates a global shortage of 44 million teachers, but the shortage headline masks a deeper equity problem: qualified teachers concentrate in urban areas and wealthier districts, leaving rural communities with unqualified, absent, or temporary teachers. Service Delivery Indicator surveys document 20-40 percent lower teacher-assessment scores in rural schools. Without verified deployment-equity analysis, teacher-deployment policies and rural-incentive programs cannot be evidence-targeted where they would most improve learning outcomes.

success_metrics.target: Every teacher-distribution claim documents EMIS data quality, update frequency, and public accessibility for the analyzed country.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against EMIS data-quality documentation.

success_metrics.target: No teacher-quality claim is merged without documenting that teacher qualification is a proxy for teaching effectiveness and that the proxy-to-effectiveness relationship is weak in many contexts.
Every deployment-gap claim links qualification distribution to learning-outcome data or explicitly documents the absence of such linkage.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms qualification-to-learning linkage or evidence-gap documentation.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, qualification-proxy safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
